• Main Page
  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples

Simulator Control (arch/sim.h)
[Tile Architecture Headers]

Detailed Description

An API for controlling the simulator at runtime.

The simulator's behavior can be modified while it is running. For example, human-readable trace output can be enabled and disabled around code of interest.

There are two ways to modify simulator behavior: programmatically, by calling various sim_* functions, and interactively, by entering commands like "sim set functional true" at the tile-monitor prompt. Typing "sim help" at that prompt provides a list of interactive commands.

All interactive commands can also be executed programmatically by passing a string to the sim_command function.

Defines

#define SIM_TRACE_CYCLES   0x01
 Enable --trace-cycle when passed to simulator_set_tracing().
#define SIM_TRACE_ROUTER   0x02
 Enable --trace-router when passed to simulator_set_tracing().
#define SIM_TRACE_REGISTER_WRITES   0x04
 Enable --trace-register-writes when passed to simulator_set_tracing().
#define SIM_TRACE_DISASM   0x08
 Enable --trace-disasm when passed to simulator_set_tracing().
#define SIM_TRACE_STALL_INFO   0x10
 Enable --trace-stall-info when passed to simulator_set_tracing().
#define SIM_TRACE_MEMORY_CONTROLLER   0x20
 Enable --trace-memory-controller when passed to simulator_set_tracing().
#define SIM_TRACE_L2_CACHE   0x40
 Enable --trace-l2 when passed to simulator_set_tracing().
#define SIM_TRACE_LINES   0x80
 Enable --trace-lines when passed to simulator_set_tracing().
#define SIM_TRACE_NONE   0
 Turn off all tracing when passed to simulator_set_tracing().
#define SIM_TRACE_ALL   (-1)
 Turn on all tracing when passed to simulator_set_tracing().
#define SIM_DUMP_REGS   0x001
 Dump the general-purpose registers.
#define SIM_DUMP_SPRS   0x002
 Dump the SPRs.
#define SIM_DUMP_ITLB   0x004
 Dump the ITLB.
#define SIM_DUMP_DTLB   0x008
 Dump the DTLB.
#define SIM_DUMP_L1I   0x010
 Dump the L1 I-cache.
#define SIM_DUMP_L1D   0x020
 Dump the L1 D-cache.
#define SIM_DUMP_L2   0x040
 Dump the L2 cache.
#define SIM_DUMP_SNREGS   0x080
 Dump the switch registers.
#define SIM_DUMP_SNITLB   0x100
 Dump the switch ITLB.
#define SIM_DUMP_SNL1I   0x200
 Dump the switch L1 I-cache.
#define SIM_DUMP_BACKTRACE   0x400
 Dump the current backtrace.
#define SIM_DUMP_VALID_LINES   0x800
 Only dump valid lines in caches.
#define SIM_DUMP_ALL   (-1 & ~SIM_DUMP_VALID_LINES)
 Dump everything that is dumpable.
#define SIM_CHIP_MEMCTL   0x001
 Use with SIM_PROFILER_CHIP_xxx to control the memory controllers.
#define SIM_CHIP_XAUI   0x002
 Use with SIM_PROFILER_CHIP_xxx to control the XAUI interface.
#define SIM_CHIP_PCIE   0x004
 Use with SIM_PROFILER_CHIP_xxx to control the PCIe interface.
#define SIM_CHIP_MPIPE   0x008
 Use with SIM_PROFILER_CHIP_xxx to control the mPIPE interface.
#define SIM_CHIP_TRIO   0x010
 Use with SIM_PROFILER_CHIP_xxx to control the TRIO interface.
#define SIM_CHIP_ALL   (-1)
 Reference all chip devices.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_GBE_0   0x0
 Change the gbe 0 bitrate.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_GBE_1   0x1
 Change the gbe 1 bitrate.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_GBE_2   0x2
 Change the gbe 2 bitrate.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_GBE_3   0x3
 Change the gbe 3 bitrate.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_XGBE_0   0x4
 Change the xgbe 0 bitrate.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_XGBE_1   0x5
 Change the xgbe 1 bitrate.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_TYPE_BITS   2
 The type of shaping to do.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_MULTIPLIER   0
 Control the multiplier.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_PPS   1
 Control the PPS.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_BPS   2
 Control the BPS.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_BITS   2
 The number of bits for the units for the shaping parameter.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_SINGLE   0
 Provide a number in single units.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_KILO   1
 Provide a number in kilo units.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_MEGA   2
 Provide a number in mega units.
#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_GIGA   3
 Provide a number in giga units.

Functions

int sim_is_simulator (void)
 Return true if the current program is running under a simulator, rather than on real hardware.
void sim_checkpoint (void)
 Checkpoint the simulator state to a checkpoint file.
unsigned int sim_get_tracing (void)
 Report whether or not various kinds of simulator tracing are enabled.
void sim_set_tracing (unsigned int mask)
 Turn on or off different kinds of simulator tracing.
void sim_dump (unsigned int mask)
 Request dumping of different kinds of simulator state.
void sim_print (const char *str)
 Print a string to the simulator stdout.
void sim_print_string (const char *str)
 Print a string to the simulator stdout.
void sim_command (const char *str)
 Execute a simulator command string.
int sim_set_shaping (unsigned shim, unsigned type, unsigned units, unsigned rate)
 Modify the shaping parameters of a shim.
void sim_enable_mpipe_links (unsigned mpipe, unsigned long link_mask)
 Enable a set of mPIPE links.
void sim_disable_mpipe_links (unsigned mpipe, unsigned long link_mask)
 Disable a set of mPIPE links.
void sim_profiler_enable (void)
 Turn profiling on for the current task.
void sim_profiler_disable (void)
 Turn profiling off for the current task.
void sim_profiler_set_enabled (int enabled)
 Turn profiling on or off for the current task.
int sim_profiler_is_enabled (void)
 Return true if and only if profiling is currently enabled for the current task.
void sim_profiler_clear (void)
 Reset profiling counters to zero for the current task.
void sim_profiler_chip_enable (unsigned int mask)
 Enable specified chip-level profiling counters.
void sim_profiler_chip_disable (unsigned int mask)
 Disable specified chip-level profiling counters.
void sim_profiler_chip_clear (unsigned int mask)
 Reset specified chip-level profiling counters to zero.
void sim_set_vcpu (int vcpu)
 Set vCPU number for a given task.
void sim_clear_vcpu (void)
 Clear vCPU status for a given task.

Define Documentation

#define SIM_TRACE_CYCLES   0x01

Enable --trace-cycle when passed to simulator_set_tracing().

#define SIM_TRACE_ROUTER   0x02

Enable --trace-router when passed to simulator_set_tracing().

#define SIM_TRACE_REGISTER_WRITES   0x04

Enable --trace-register-writes when passed to simulator_set_tracing().

#define SIM_TRACE_DISASM   0x08

Enable --trace-disasm when passed to simulator_set_tracing().

#define SIM_TRACE_STALL_INFO   0x10

Enable --trace-stall-info when passed to simulator_set_tracing().

#define SIM_TRACE_MEMORY_CONTROLLER   0x20

Enable --trace-memory-controller when passed to simulator_set_tracing().

#define SIM_TRACE_L2_CACHE   0x40

Enable --trace-l2 when passed to simulator_set_tracing().

#define SIM_TRACE_LINES   0x80

Enable --trace-lines when passed to simulator_set_tracing().

#define SIM_TRACE_NONE   0

Turn off all tracing when passed to simulator_set_tracing().

#define SIM_TRACE_ALL   (-1)

Turn on all tracing when passed to simulator_set_tracing().

#define SIM_DUMP_REGS   0x001

Dump the general-purpose registers.

#define SIM_DUMP_SPRS   0x002

Dump the SPRs.

#define SIM_DUMP_ITLB   0x004

Dump the ITLB.

#define SIM_DUMP_DTLB   0x008

Dump the DTLB.

#define SIM_DUMP_L1I   0x010

Dump the L1 I-cache.

#define SIM_DUMP_L1D   0x020

Dump the L1 D-cache.

#define SIM_DUMP_L2   0x040

Dump the L2 cache.

#define SIM_DUMP_SNREGS   0x080

Dump the switch registers.

#define SIM_DUMP_SNITLB   0x100

Dump the switch ITLB.

#define SIM_DUMP_SNL1I   0x200

Dump the switch L1 I-cache.

#define SIM_DUMP_BACKTRACE   0x400

Dump the current backtrace.

#define SIM_DUMP_VALID_LINES   0x800

Only dump valid lines in caches.

#define SIM_DUMP_ALL   (-1 & ~SIM_DUMP_VALID_LINES)

Dump everything that is dumpable.

#define SIM_CHIP_MEMCTL   0x001

Use with SIM_PROFILER_CHIP_xxx to control the memory controllers.

#define SIM_CHIP_XAUI   0x002

Use with SIM_PROFILER_CHIP_xxx to control the XAUI interface.

#define SIM_CHIP_PCIE   0x004

Use with SIM_PROFILER_CHIP_xxx to control the PCIe interface.

#define SIM_CHIP_MPIPE   0x008

Use with SIM_PROFILER_CHIP_xxx to control the mPIPE interface.

#define SIM_CHIP_TRIO   0x010

Use with SIM_PROFILER_CHIP_xxx to control the TRIO interface.

#define SIM_CHIP_ALL   (-1)

Reference all chip devices.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_GBE_0   0x0

Change the gbe 0 bitrate.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_GBE_1   0x1

Change the gbe 1 bitrate.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_GBE_2   0x2

Change the gbe 2 bitrate.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_GBE_3   0x3

Change the gbe 3 bitrate.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_XGBE_0   0x4

Change the xgbe 0 bitrate.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_XGBE_1   0x5

Change the xgbe 1 bitrate.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_TYPE_BITS   2

The type of shaping to do.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_MULTIPLIER   0

Control the multiplier.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_PPS   1

Control the PPS.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_BPS   2

Control the BPS.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_BITS   2

The number of bits for the units for the shaping parameter.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_SINGLE   0

Provide a number in single units.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_KILO   1

Provide a number in kilo units.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_MEGA   2

Provide a number in mega units.

#define SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_GIGA   3

Provide a number in giga units.

Function Documentation

int sim_is_simulator ( void   ) 

Return true if the current program is running under a simulator, rather than on real hardware.

If running on hardware, other "sim_xxx()" calls have no useful effect.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

void sim_checkpoint ( void   ) 

Checkpoint the simulator state to a checkpoint file.

The checkpoint file name is either the default or the name specified on the command line with "--checkpoint-file".

unsigned int sim_get_tracing ( void   ) 

Report whether or not various kinds of simulator tracing are enabled.

Returns:
The bitwise OR of these values:
SIM_TRACE_CYCLES (--trace-cycles), SIM_TRACE_ROUTER (--trace-router), SIM_TRACE_REGISTER_WRITES (--trace-register-writes), SIM_TRACE_DISASM (--trace-disasm), SIM_TRACE_STALL_INFO (--trace-stall-info) SIM_TRACE_MEMORY_CONTROLLER (--trace-memory-controller) SIM_TRACE_L2_CACHE (--trace-l2) SIM_TRACE_LINES (--trace-lines)

void sim_set_tracing ( unsigned int  mask  ) 

Turn on or off different kinds of simulator tracing.

Parameters:
mask Either one of these special values:
SIM_TRACE_NONE (turns off tracing), SIM_TRACE_ALL (turns on all possible tracing).

or the bitwise OR of these values:

SIM_TRACE_CYCLES (--trace-cycles), SIM_TRACE_ROUTER (--trace-router), SIM_TRACE_REGISTER_WRITES (--trace-register-writes), SIM_TRACE_DISASM (--trace-disasm), SIM_TRACE_STALL_INFO (--trace-stall-info) SIM_TRACE_MEMORY_CONTROLLER (--trace-memory-controller) SIM_TRACE_L2_CACHE (--trace-l2) SIM_TRACE_LINES (--trace-lines)

void sim_dump ( unsigned int  mask  ) 

Request dumping of different kinds of simulator state.

Parameters:
mask Either this special value:
SIM_DUMP_ALL (dump all known state)

or the bitwise OR of these values:

SIM_DUMP_REGS (the register file), SIM_DUMP_SPRS (the SPRs), SIM_DUMP_ITLB (the iTLB), SIM_DUMP_DTLB (the dTLB), SIM_DUMP_L1I (the L1 I-cache), SIM_DUMP_L1D (the L1 D-cache), SIM_DUMP_L2 (the L2 cache), SIM_DUMP_SNREGS (the switch register file), SIM_DUMP_SNITLB (the switch iTLB), SIM_DUMP_SNL1I (the switch L1 I-cache), SIM_DUMP_BACKTRACE (the current backtrace)

void sim_print ( const char *  str  ) 

Print a string to the simulator stdout.

Parameters:
str The string to be written.
Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

void sim_print_string ( const char *  str  ) 

Print a string to the simulator stdout.

Parameters:
str The string to be written (a newline is automatically added).

void sim_command ( const char *  str  ) 

Execute a simulator command string.

Type 'sim help' at the tile-monitor prompt to learn what commands are available. Note the use of the tile-monitor "sim" command to pass commands to the simulator.

The argument to sim_command() does not include the leading "sim" prefix used at the tile-monitor prompt; for example, you might call sim_command("trace disasm").

int sim_set_shaping ( unsigned  shim,
unsigned  type,
unsigned  units,
unsigned  rate 
)

Modify the shaping parameters of a shim.

Parameters:
shim The shim to modify. One of: SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_GBE_0 SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_GBE_1 SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_GBE_2 SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_GBE_3 SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_XGBE_0 SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_XGBE_1
type The type of shaping. This should be the same type of shaping that is already in place on the shim. One of: SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_MULTIPLIER SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_PPS SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_BPS
units The magnitude of the rate. One of: SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_SINGLE SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_KILO SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_MEGA SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_UNITS_GIGA
rate The rate to which to change it. This must fit in SIM_CONTROL_SHAPING_RATE_BITS bits or a warning is issued and the shaping is not changed.
Returns:
0 if no problems were detected in the arguments to sim_set_shaping or 1 if problems were detected (for example, rate does not fit in 17 bits).

void sim_enable_mpipe_links ( unsigned  mpipe,
unsigned long  link_mask 
)

Enable a set of mPIPE links.

Pass a -1 link_mask to enable all links.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, mpipe/ingress/app.c, mpipe/loopback/app.c, and mpipe/multi_rules/app.c.

void sim_disable_mpipe_links ( unsigned  mpipe,
unsigned long  link_mask 
)

Disable a set of mPIPE links.

Pass a -1 link_mask to disable all links.

void sim_profiler_enable ( void   ) 

Turn profiling on for the current task.

Note that this has no effect if run in an environment without profiling support (thus, the proper flags to the simulator must be supplied).

void sim_profiler_disable ( void   ) 

Turn profiling off for the current task.

void sim_profiler_set_enabled ( int  enabled  ) 

Turn profiling on or off for the current task.

Parameters:
enabled If true, turns on profiling. If false, turns it off.
Note that this has no effect if run in an environment without profiling support (thus, the proper flags to the simulator must be supplied).

int sim_profiler_is_enabled ( void   ) 

Return true if and only if profiling is currently enabled for the current task.

This returns false even if sim_profiler_enable() was called if the current execution environment does not support profiling.

void sim_profiler_clear ( void   ) 

Reset profiling counters to zero for the current task.

Resetting can be done while profiling is enabled. It does not affect the chip-wide profiling counters.

void sim_profiler_chip_enable ( unsigned int  mask  ) 

Enable specified chip-level profiling counters.

Does not affect the per-task profiling counters.

Parameters:
mask Either this special value:
SIM_CHIP_ALL (enables all chip-level components).

or the bitwise OR of these values:

SIM_CHIP_MEMCTL (enable all memory controllers) SIM_CHIP_XAUI (enable all XAUI controllers) SIM_CHIP_MPIPE (enable all MPIPE controllers)

void sim_profiler_chip_disable ( unsigned int  mask  ) 

Disable specified chip-level profiling counters.

Does not affect the per-task profiling counters.

Parameters:
mask Either this special value:
SIM_CHIP_ALL (disables all chip-level components).

or the bitwise OR of these values:

SIM_CHIP_MEMCTL (disable all memory controllers) SIM_CHIP_XAUI (disable all XAUI controllers) SIM_CHIP_MPIPE (disable all MPIPE controllers)

void sim_profiler_chip_clear ( unsigned int  mask  ) 

Reset specified chip-level profiling counters to zero.

Does not affect the per-task profiling counters.

Parameters:
mask Either this special value:
SIM_CHIP_ALL (clears all chip-level components).

or the bitwise OR of these values:

SIM_CHIP_MEMCTL (clear all memory controllers) SIM_CHIP_XAUI (clear all XAUI controllers) SIM_CHIP_MPIPE (clear all MPIPE controllers)

void sim_set_vcpu ( int  vcpu  ) 

Set vCPU number for a given task.

Parameters:
vcpu Virtual cpu to set.

void sim_clear_vcpu ( void   ) 

Clear vCPU status for a given task.
